questions.question: What is a subpage in Notion?

options.explanation: A shared database view is a way to display data, but a subpage refers to the page structure itself.
A subpage is defined as a page located inside another page, allowing for hierarchical organization.
A user account grants access to the workspace, not a page structure.
A file attachment is content, not a page structure.

questions.question: How can you create a new subpage inside an existing page?

options.explanation: The /database command creates a database block, not a new page.
Using the slash command /page creates a new subpage within the currently open page.
The trash icon is used to delete content, not create new pages.
The Settings menu is for configuration, not for creating new pages.

questions.question: How do you remove a subpage from its parent page?

options.text: Drag the page to the sidebar
options.explanation: Dragging to the sidebar does not remove it from the parent; you must drag it out of the parent's toggle.

options.text: Click on it in the sidebar and drag it out of the parent page's toggle
options.explanation: Dragging the page out of the parent's toggle detaches it, effectively removing it from that parent structure.
Deleting the parent page would delete the subpage as well, which is not the intended action for just rearranging.
Copying and pasting creates a duplicate, it does not remove the original from the parent page.

questions.question: Which visual element indicates the current page's location relative to its parent pages?

options.explanation: The sidebar shows the hierarchy of pages, but it does not display the current path at the top of the view.
Breadcrumbs appear at the top of the page and show the path of navigation, indicating where the current page lives.
The page title indicates the name of the current page, not its location in the hierarchy.
The footer typically contains copyright or sharing links, not navigation breadcrumbs.

questions.question: How can you create a subpage without first opening the parent page?

options.explanation: The slash command /page requires the parent page to be open to create a new page inside it.

options.text: Click the + next to the parent page in the sidebar
options.explanation: Clicking the `+` next to a parent page in the sidebar creates a subpage immediately without needing to open the parent view.
Importing a file typically adds it as a new page at the root level, not as a subpage of a specific parent.
Creating a new workspace starts from scratch and does not allow specifying a parent page directly.

questions.question: If you want to make an existing page a subpage of another page, how do you do it?

options.text: Drag the page to the sidebar
options.explanation: Dragging to the sidebar does not automatically attach it to a specific parent page.

options.text: Drag the page into another page's toggle
options.explanation: Dragging an existing page into another page's toggle in the sidebar will make it a subpage of that page.
Copying and pasting creates a duplicate; the original page remains in its original location.

options.text: Select 'Move to...' from the right-click menu
options.explanation: While moving is possible, the sidebar drag-and-drop method is the primary mechanism described for rearranging page structure.

questions.question: After creating a new subpage, where can you find options to format the page using a template or table?

options.explanation: The top toolbar contains global tools and page settings, not the quick-formatting options for the new page.
At the bottom of the new page, there are options to import, use templates, or create tables to format the page immediately.
The sidebar is used for navigation and rearranging, not for formatting the content of a specific page.
The header menu is typically reserved for page sharing and toggling views.

questions.question: When viewing a page in the sidebar, how are its subpages displayed initially?

options.text: Always visible in the main view
options.explanation: Subpages are not visible in the main view immediately; they are hidden inside the parent page's toggle.
The sidebar displays subpages inside a toggle for the parent page, keeping the view organized.

options.text: As a separate list at the top
options.explanation: Subpages are not listed at the top; they are nested within their parent.
Breadcrumbs show the path, but do not display the list of subpages in the sidebar.

questions.question: What is true regarding the number of subpages you can have for a single page?

options.text: You can only have one subpage
options.explanation: Notion allows for any number of subpages for a single page.

options.text: There is no limit on the number of subpages per page.

options.explanation: You can create as many subpages as needed within a parent page.

options.text: You can only have subpages if you are on a paid plan
options.explanation: Creating subpages is a core feature available to all Notion users.

questions.question: When you click the + next to a parent page in the sidebar, what happens immediately?

options.text: The new page opens in the sidebar
options.explanation: The new page opens in the main view, not the sidebar.

options.text: The new page opens in the main view
options.explanation: Clicking the + creates a subpage that immediately opens in the main view of the app.
The sidebar remains open and functional.
The parent page remains intact.
